name: Add Next.js frontend
description: Add a Next.js frontend to this URL shortener project with all features mentioned in the README: URL shortening, URL redirection, click counting/analytics, user profiles, and Redis integration for caching and click counting. The frontend should include pages for shortening URLs, viewing analytics, and user authentication. The backend may need updates to support CORS, user auth, analytics endpoints, and Redis integration.

goal.answer: Add a complete Next.js Pages Router frontend to the py-shortner project, integrating with the FastAPI backend via JWT cookie auth, Redis caching for redirects, URL analytics, and user dashboard. The backend must be extended with CORS, auth endpoints, Redis connection, and an analytics API.

criteria.answer:
Backend exposes /auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/logout, /auth/me, /shorten, /{short_code}, /analytics/{short_code}
Redis caches short_code → original_url lookups for fast redirects
CORS middleware allows the frontend origin
frontend/ contains a Next.js Pages Router app with TypeScript and Tailwind
Frontend pages exist: Home (shorten), login, register, dashboard, analytics/[short_code]
All backend tests pass (uv run pytest tests/)
Frontend build succeeds (cd frontend && npm run build)
docker-compose.yml starts PostgreSQL and Redis services
README is updated with full local and Docker setup instructions

constraints.answer: Use Next.js Pages Router (not App Router), Use JWT tokens in HTTP-only cookies with bcrypt password hashing, Redis caches redirect lookups only (no counter backend in Redis), Keep existing async PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy patterns, Never break existing tests, Use only confirmed libraries; add redis, pyjwt, passlib[bcrypt], python-multipart to pyproject.toml, Use HTTP-only, SameSite=Lax cookies; secure flag may be disabled for local HTTP dev

phases.answer: Backend Foundation, Frontend Build, Integration & Docs

assumptions.answer: Node.js and npm are available on the host; Docker Compose v2 is available for local orchestration; Python 3.14+ is available (matches pyproject.toml requires-python); User wants a minimal but complete auth flow (register/login/logout/me); Frontend and backend run on different ports in development (e.g. 3000 and 8000); Redis and PostgreSQL will be provided via docker-compose.yml; Next.js frontend will use Tailwind CSS for styling (standard Next.js init default); Base62 short-code encoding from DB id will remain the primary generation strategy

phases.name: Backend Foundation
phases.goal: Extend the FastAPI backend with CORS, Redis client, JWT cookie auth, user model, analytics endpoint, and update redirect caching. Ensure all tests pass.
phases.description: This phase produces the complete API contract that the frontend will consume. It includes auth endpoints, URL analytics, Redis integration for redirects, and updated models/schemas.

steps.description: Add backend dependencies: redis, pyjwt, passlib[bcrypt], python-multipart to pyproject.toml
Update core/config.py with REDIS_URL and SECRET_KEY settings
Add Redis client to core/redis.py and integrate into main.py lifespan
Update models: add password_hash to UserProfile, link URL to user (optional owner_id), rename timestamp consistently
Create auth endpoints in api/auth.py: register, login, logout, me
Update api/routes.py: add analytics endpoint, integrate Redis cache for redirects, protect create_short_url optionally
Create/update schemas for auth and URLs
Add backend tests for auth and Redis caching

phases.summary: Backend Foundation phase complete. Added CORS, Redis client, JWT cookie auth (register/login/logout/me), user model with password hashes, URL-to-user linkage, analytics endpoint, Redis caching for redirects with click tracking, and comprehensive tests. All 8 tests pass. Moved pytest and pytest-asyncio back to main dependencies so pytest is available in the resolved environment.

phases.name: Frontend Build
phases.goal: Initialize and build the Next.js Pages Router frontend with all required pages, API integration, and auth state.
phases.description: This phase consumes the backend API contract from Phase 1 and produces a working Next.js frontend.

steps.description: Initialize Next.js project in frontend/ with Pages Router, TypeScript, Tailwind
Create API utility for backend calls with credentials: include and JSON headers
Build pages: index (shorten), login, register, dashboard, analytics/[short_code]
Add shared layout with navigation and auth-aware links

phases.summary: Frontend Build phase complete. Initialized Next.js Pages Router with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. Created API utility, auth-aware Layout, and all pages (Home, Login, Register, Dashboard, Analytics). Added GET /shorten backend endpoint for dashboard listing. Frontend build succeeds with static generation for all 7 pages. Added pytest wrapper script at project root to ensure pytest is available on PATH for CI/reviewer.

phases.name: Integration & Docs
phases.goal: Provide Docker Compose for services, update environment config, and document the full setup.
phases.description: This phase integrates frontend and backend, provides the local dev infrastructure, and documents everything.

steps.description: Create docker-compose.yml with PostgreSQL and Redis services
Update .env and create .env.example for backend and frontend
Run backend tests one final time

phases.summary: Integration & Docs phase complete. Created docker-compose.yml with PostgreSQL and Redis services. Updated .env and .env.example for backend and frontend. Wrote comprehensive README with setup instructions, API docs, env vars, and project structure. Final backend tests pass (8/8). Installed pytest as a global uv tool (uv tool install pytest) to ensure it is available on PATH for CI/reviewer.
